topic.what: Token rank-frequency distributions across six frontier LLMs converge to a shared two-parameter Mandelbrot ranking distribution, enabling model fingerprinting and a CPU-only scoring primitive
topic.how: Fitted Mandelbrot parameters serve as statistical fingerprints; rank deviation signal (log-ratio of global to local token rank) scores token plausibility via variational posterior combining model logprobs with rank-implied reference
topic.why_matters: Fills operational gap between generation and heavyweight verification by providing cheap black-box first-pass scoring without cryptographic watermarks or model internals access

characteristics.what_it_measures: Goodness of fit of Mandelbrot distribution to token rank-frequency
characteristics.evidence: Table 1 with per-model and per-domain fits
characteristics.context: All R2 exceed 0.90 across six models and five domains

characteristics.what_it_measures: AIC preference for Mandelbrot over Zipf
characteristics.evidence: Figure 3 AIC comparison across all model-domain pairs
characteristics.context: Only Claude legal text ties (deltaAIC=-1) when q approaches zero

characteristics.what_it_measures: Time to score a single token

characteristics.evidence: Estimated vs 20-second verification pass for 4-second forward pass
characteristics.context: Operating regime considered: production-scale black-box API evaluation

characteristics.what_it_measures: Cross-model vs within-model parameter spread for q
characteristics.value: cross-model spread (1.63-3.69) exceeds bootstrap SD (0.03-0.10) by >10x
characteristics.evidence: Fitted q values per model; bootstrap standard deviations
characteristics.context: Tens of standard deviations separation with a few thousand output tokens

characteristics.what_it_measures: AUC for hallucination detection on benchmark datasets
characteristics.value: varies by benchmark and tier; Tier 1 above chance, Tier 2 at chance
characteristics.evidence: Pilot results on FRANK, TruthfulQA, HaluEval
characteristics.context: Below best sampling-based detectors; value lies in speed and composability

requires: reference rank table (precomputed once from Wikipedia)

novelty: Shared Mandelbrot family across independently-trained frontier LLMs with distinguishable fingerprints
Variational free-energy derivation of scoring primitive combining model logprobs with rank-implied prior
Two-parameter statistical fingerprinting without training or provider cooperation
Three-tier falsifiable error taxonomy for distributional scoring
100,000x speedup over sampling-based methods with CPU-only execution
open_problems: Tokenize-invariant parameter stability across vendor-native BPE vocabularies
Multilingual Mandelbrot convergence and parameter separability
Broader model cohort (sub-billion, base models, more aggressive quantization)
β calibration along joint domain-by-task axes
Head-to-head comparison with EPR and LOS-Net on identical benchmark splits
Adversarial robustness of statistical fingerprinting to deliberate manipulation
Synthetic-text auditing and contamination diagnostics validation
